summary: Iteration 4 activation feature tested end-to-end (backend + frontend). All 8 backend pytest tests pass; frontend flow /activation -> form submit -> /activation/thanks works. Chrome hiding logic works correctly (main Navbar/Footer hidden on /activation and /activation/thanks; visible on /, /products). Nav 'Activate' link present on non-activation pages. Regression: /products still lists 20 products.

issues: Empty-submit toast 'Please complete all fields' cannot fire because inputs use HTML5 required attribute, so the browser blocks submit before the JS validation runs. Not a functional bug but the spec item cannot be reached. Consider removing 'required' from inputs OR marking this as expected (browser native tooltip).

action_items: Optional: Decide whether to keep HTML5 'required' (browser-native validation) or remove it to allow the 'Please complete all fields' toast to fire. Current UX is safe either way.
Optional: On thanks page 'Chat now' has no chat widget wired — falls back to mailto:info@buyinstantkeys.com. Working as designed.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1059 lines — consider splitting into routers (orders, admin, activation, coupons, banner) for maintainability.
PATCH /api/admin/activations/{id} accepts a raw dict — consider Pydantic model for input validation.
Activation POST awaits two send_email calls sequentially — could use asyncio.gather or fire-and-forget to reduce latency (currently 1.5-2s response time). Best-effort catching is fine.
seed_data_creation: Created a few TEST_* activation records via API (not cleaned; harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 4 fully green. Activation feature (POST /api/activations, GET/PATCH /api/admin/activations) verified. Frontend chrome-hiding logic on /activation* verified. HTML5 required + JS toast validation coexist; browser-native validation fires first for empty submits.
